title: Level 83
wikitext:
{{Audio|=yes|file=83.mp3}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:'''LEVEL 83: Field of Roses'''}} {{Background|file=Level 83- The Field of Roses.webp}} __NOTOC__ {{Alt Class 2|exit=4|environment=1|entity=0|no=83}}
'''Level 83''' is the 84th level of the Backrooms.
---- {{Blurimage|align=center|file=Rose-Sunrise-83.jpg|caption=Eternal Sunrise}} ==Description== '''Level 83''' is a sprawling field of red roses under a dull blue sky. Plantlife is sustained by hot winds that carry moisture and other chemicals necessary for their survival. These compounds are contained within clouds, which conform to typical cloud classification despite the additional materials. These same winds scatter the sweet smell of trillions of roses, making it much more bearable. The winds keep the climate of '''Level 83''' humid and around a relatively stable 25°C.There have been a few reports of the temperature going down to 20°C as one travels east, but this has yet to be replicated. Despite the frequent presence of dark clouds in the sky, there have been no reports of rain. The only noted weather phenomenon is a very thin fog that almost always blankets '''Level 83'''. To the east, there is a bright light just above the horizon that gives the eastern sky the appearance of sunrise. This light is similar to the Sun in the Frontrooms, but it emits significantly reduced light and heat. It can also be safely observed for prolonged periods, which some describe as a relaxing experience. It is stationary and is the only natural light source in '''Level 83'''.

The terrain of '''Level 83''' is often very flat, and although hills and dips exist, they are usually small and gradual. It is very easy to navigate, with the only real obstacles being small trees that often clump up together, usually as windbreaks. The ground is composed of packed silt with hydrophobic properties, making it infertile. Plants can only exist due to the aforementioned wind. The dirt itself seems to extend down indefinitely with no rock layer ever being found. Small dirt pathways can infrequently be found, which can be followed to various exits. These paths are indistinguishable from other earth material in '''Level 83''' and may rarely intersect. No other signs of development have been documented, either natural or by groups.

Roses within '''Level 83''' are an unknown type of red rose only found within the level. They are biologically similar to regular roses but have smaller root systemsRoots only exist to anchor the rose since the soil is devoid of nutrients. and produce a liquid substance called 'Rose Blood' instead of glucose. Beyond Rose Blood, only [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] can be found. It is entirely devoid of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and other foods and liquids, making it impossible to remain on without external supplies. The thorns of roses, alongside tall grasses that also grow on '''Level 83''' can make navigating uncomfortable without long pants. Roses are not the only type of flower, as red poppies are also frequent, but do not produce Rose Blood. Bushes of varying sizes can frequently be seen growing. On rare occasions, wanderers may also encounter patches of wheat. These plants, the hydrophobic soil, and the easy entrance into '''Level 83''' from [[Level 10]] suggests a connection.Before the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]], '''Level 83''' was considered a sub-level of Level 10 by many. {{Blurimage|file=Rose-hill-83.jpg|align=center|caption=Rolling Hills}} ==Hallucinations== There have been numerous reports of shadowy figures staring either at wanderers or the sun. These figures are typically hazy and shadowy and tower over the fields. They most often are seen towards the east and vanish when noticed. These figures are typically indistinct but may resemble humanoids or writhing masses. They are typically partially obscured by the fog and may appear to loom out of it. Wanderers frequently report feelings of being watched or the presence of eyes on the figures. It is believed that these figures are simply a common hallucination brought on as a property of the level, as they never seem to come closer than the horizon. Photographic efforts to prove or disprove their existence have been fruitless, as the property of disappearing prevents traditional photography, and they seem not to manifest around video equipment. Despite rumors, the heightened disappearance rate on '''Level 83''' compared to other [[:Category:Class 1|Class 1]] Levels is considered a byproduct of a lack of naturally occurring food, water, or other humans to seek help from. {{Blurimage|file=Rose-blood.jpg|align=center|caption=2 Jars of Rose Blood.}} ==Rose Blood== Rose Blood is a glucose alternative produced by roses native to '''Level 83'''. It flows through the flower's vascular system and can be collected via 'wringing' the flower out. Roses that produce it transpire it instead of regular water, meaning it can collect on their thorns. This can cause mild sickness if it gets into the bloodstream. It is liquid instead of solid and provides energy to the plant through an unknown process. It is pink in color and possesses a PH of 2.2. If drunk, it has a sakura-like flavor. Drinking it may cause tooth decay and gastrointestinal problems. Drinking it will also trigger hallucinogenic effects. Onset hallucinations often contain one of a few reoccurring elements, including but not limited to, thorny red tendrils that wrap around wanderers, red petals falling out of the clouds, roses and the sun being replaced with human-like eyes, and the sensation of being pulled to the east. The one element guaranteed to occur is a sudden silence. It is believed that the shadowy hallucinations seen in '''Level 83''' are due to an airborne compound of Rose Blood, but this has yet to be replicated. {{Blurimage|file=Foggy-rosefield-83.jpg|align=center|caption=Heavy Fog}} ==Entities== '''Level 83''' is believed to be devoid of entities. Shadowy figures are considered to be a collective hallucination instead of actual entities. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to a lack of [[Object 1|Almond Water]], forming permanent settlements on '''Level 83''' is considered not worthwhile. The only available resource is Rose Water, which does not require a lengthy stay to harvest. ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Wandering far enough in [[Level 10]] may result in a gradual and subtle transition into '''Level 83'''. *While wandering in [[Level 416]], a bright light may appear to the east. Turning to face this light results in sudden transportation to '''Level 83'''. *Some pathways in the Garden Area of [[Level 798]] may open up to the fields of '''Level 83'''. *Noclipping through a poster depicting a rose in [[Level 679]] ===Exits=== *Following dirt pathways for long stretches frequently leads to forced noclips to other outdoor, and usually safe, levels. *Following Car Trails will instead lead to a transition into the fields of [[Level 180]]. This exit does not take as much time to transport wanderers as regular dirt pathways do. *On occasion, the dark silhouettes of buildings may appear on the horizon. Unlike most hallucinations, these buildings do not disappear when noticed, and walking towards them leads to [[Level 84]]. *Out of place yellow flowers can rarely be encountered which will send one to [[Level 232]] when interacted with. {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Rewrite By''': [[User:Mycatcaughtamoth|Mycatcaughtamoth]]
